message.content:
Create the following 4 page components AND the main App.tsx in C:\Users\User\stan-io\. Each page must use CSS variables and import from the project's existing UI components and demo data.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT:
- Project location: C:\Users\User\stan-io
- UI components: Button, Card, Badge, StatsCard, DataTable, Tabs, EmptyState, Modal, Toggle (all in ../components/ui/ or ../../components/ui/)
- Demo data: demoUser, demoIntegrations (from ../lib/demo-data or ./lib/demo-data)
- Types from ../types or ./types: Integration, User, StoreTheme
- Use Tailwind CSS with CSS custom properties
- Icons from lucide-react
- Every page component is a default export

## PART A: Create 4 pages in pages/ directory

### 1. pages/StoreSettings.tsx
- Page title "Store Settings"
- Store URL section: shows stan.io/sarahcreates with copy button
- Store info form: Store Name input, Bio textarea, Profile Image upload area (drag & drop placeholder)
- Social links section: inputs for each social platform (instagram, tiktok, youtube, twitter, facebook, linkedin, pinterest) with platform icon and URL input
- SEO section: meta title input, meta description textarea
- Custom domain section: input for custom domain + instructions text
- "Save Changes" button at bottom

### 2. pages/Integrations.tsx
- Page title "Integrations"
- Explain: "Connect your favorite tools to extend your store's capabilities"
- Integration grid (2 columns): each demoIntegration as a Card showing icon (use a lucide icon matching the icon field: CreditCard, Wallet, Calendar, Mail, BarChart, Zap), name, description, connection status badge (connected = success, not connected = default), Connect/Disconnect button
- Filter by type: All, Payment, Calendar, Email, Analytics
- Each connected integration shows a green "Connected" badge and "Disconnect" button (danger variant)
- Each disconnected one shows "Connect" button (primary)

### 3. pages/PaymentSetup.tsx
- Page title "Payment Setup"
- Stripe section (Card): Stripe logo text, connection status (connected with green badge), account info "stripe_acct_xxx", "Disconnect" button. If not connected: "Connect with Stripe" primary button
- PayPal section (Card): PayPal logo text, not connected status, "Connect PayPal" primary button
- Payout settings: payout schedule dropdown (daily/weekly/monthly), minimum payout amount input, currency selector
- Tax settings section: collect taxes toggle, tax rate input (%)
- Transaction fees info: "Stan.io charges 5% on Creator plan, 0% on Creator Pro"

### 4. pages/Account.tsx
- Page title "Account"
- Profile section (Card): avatar upload area, name input, email input (disabled), username input
- Password section (Card): current password, new password, confirm password inputs, "Update Password" button
- Plan section: current plan badge (Creator Pro), features list, "Manage Plan" button, billing cycle info
- Danger Zone (Card with red border): "Delete Account" button with warning text
- Notification preferences: email notifications toggle, marketing emails toggle, weekly digest toggle

## PART B: Create App.tsx in the project root (C:\Users\User\stan-io\App.tsx)

The App.tsx is the main routing component. It must:

1. Import React, { useState, Suspense, lazy }
2. Import ThemeProvider from './lib/theme-context'
3. Import AppShell from './components/layout/AppShell'
4. Import demoUser from './lib/demo-data'
5. Lazy import ALL 19 pages:
   - Dashboard from './pages/Dashboard'
   - MyStore from './pages/MyStore'
   - AllProducts from './pages/AllProducts'
   - DigitalProducts from './pages/DigitalProducts'
   - Courses from './pages/Courses'
   - Bookings from './pages/Bookings'
   - Memberships from './pages/Memberships'
   - Orders from './pages/Orders'
   - Subscribers from './pages/Subscribers'
   - Affiliates from './pages/Affiliates'
   - EmailFlows from './pages/EmailFlows'
   - Broadcasts from './pages/Broadcasts'
   - DiscountCodes from './pages/DiscountCodes'
   - AnalyticsOverview from './pages/AnalyticsOverview'
   - TrafficSources from './pages/TrafficSources'
   - StoreSettings from './pages/StoreSettings'
   - Integrations from './pages/Integrations'
   - PaymentSetup from './pages/PaymentSetup'
   - Account from './pages/Account'

6. Create a PAGE_MAP: Record<string, React.LazyExoticComponent<any>> mapping page IDs to components:
   'dashboard' → Dashboard
   'my-store' → MyStore
   'all-products' → AllProducts
   'digital-products' → DigitalProducts
   'courses' → Courses
   'bookings' → Bookings
   'memberships' → Memberships
   'orders' → Orders
   'subscribers' → Subscribers
   'affiliates' → Affiliates
   'email-flows' → EmailFlows
   'broadcasts' → Broadcasts
   'discount-codes' → DiscountCodes
   'analytics-overview' → AnalyticsOverview
   'traffic-sources' → TrafficSources
   'store-settings' → StoreSettings
   'integrations' → Integrations
   'payment-setup' → PaymentSetup
   'account' → Account

7. The App component:
   - useState for currentView (default 'dashboard')
   - Derive ActivePage from PAGE_MAP[currentView] || Dashboard
   - Return ThemeProvider wrapping AppShell with:
     - activePage={currentView}
     - onPageChange={setCurrentView}
     - user={{ name: demoUser.name, avatar: demoUser.avatar }}
     - plan={demoUser.plan}
   - Inside AppShell: Suspense with a loading spinner fallback, then render <ActivePage />
   - The loading fallback should be a centered spinner using the accent color

8. Export default App

Create the pages/ directory if needed, then write all 5 files.

content.text: I'll start by reading the existing project structure and files to understand the codebase before creating the new files.

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { Copy, Check, Upload, Globe, Search, Save, Instagram, Youtube, Twitter, Facebook, Linkedin, ExternalLink, } from 'lucide-react'; import { Card } from '../components/ui/Card'; import { Button } from '../components/ui/Button'; import { demoUser } from '../lib/demo-data'; const SOCIAL_PLATFORMS = [ { key: 'instagram', label: 'Instagram', icon: Instagram, prefix: 'https://instagram.com/' }, { key: 'tiktok', label: 'TikTok', icon: ExternalLink, prefix: 'https://tiktok.com/@' }, { key: 'youtube', label: 'YouTube', icon: Youtube, prefix: 'https://youtube.com/@' }, { key: 'twitter', label: 'Twitter / X', icon: Twitter, prefix: 'https://twitter.com/' }, { key: 'facebook', label: 'Facebook', icon: Facebook, prefix: 'https://facebook.com/' }, { key: 'linkedin', label: 'LinkedIn', icon: Linkedin, prefix: 'https://linkedin.com/in/' }, { key: 'pinterest', label: 'Pinterest', icon: ExternalLink, prefix: 'https://pinterest.com/' }, ] as const; export default function StoreSettings() { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); const [storeName, setStoreName] = useState(demoUser.name); const [bio, setBio] = useState(demoUser.bio); const [socialLinks, setSocialLinks] = useState>({ instagram: demoUser.socialLinks.instagram || '', tiktok: demoUser.socialLinks.tiktok || '', youtube: demoUser.socialLinks.youtube || '', twitter: demoUser.socialLinks.twitter || '', facebook: demoUser.socialLinks.facebook || '', linkedin: demoUser.socialLinks.linkedin || '', pinterest: demoUser.socialLinks.pinterest || '', }); const [metaTitle, setMetaTitle] = useState(`${demoUser.name} - Creator Store`); const [metaDescription, setMetaDescription] = useState(demoUser.bio); const [customDomain, setCustomDomain] = useState(''); const storeUrl = `stan.io/${demoUser.username}`; const handleCopyUrl = () => { navigator.clipboard.writeText(`https://${storeUrl}`); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); }; const handleSocialChange = (key: string, value: string) => { setSocialLinks((prev) => ({ ...prev, [key]: value })); }; return (

content.text:
Build succeeded with all 19 pages compiled and code-split into individual chunks. All 4 new pages are visible in the output:
- `PaymentSetup-vjYbcRzp.js` (8.33 kB)
- `StoreSettings-B3Pv6GrR.js` (8.49 kB)
- `Account-s5sk2RPX.js` (9.46 kB)
- `Integrations` is included in the build as well (part of the earlier output)

Here is a summary of all 5 files created:

---

## Files Created

### 1. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\StoreSettings.tsx` (11.2 KB)
- Page title "Store Settings" with subtitle
- **Store URL section**: displays `stan.io/sarahcreates` with a copy-to-clipboard button (shows "Copied" state with check icon)
- **Store Info form**: Store Name input, Bio textarea with character counter, Profile Image drag-and-drop upload area
- **Social Links section**: 7 platform rows (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Twitter/X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Pinterest) each with platform icon and URL input, pre-populated from `demoUser.socialLinks`
- **SEO section**: meta title input with character counter, meta description textarea with character counter
- **Custom Domain section**: domain input + CNAME instructions in a highlighted info box
- **Save Changes button** at bottom right with save icon

### 2. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\Integrations.tsx` (4.3 KB)
- Page title "Integrations" with description
- **Filter tabs**: All, Payment, Calendar, Email, Analytics (using the `Tabs` component)
- **Integration grid** (2-column): renders each `demoIntegration` as a `Card` with:
  - Lucide icon matching the `icon` field via `ICON_MAP` (CreditCard, Wallet, Calendar, Mail, BarChart, Zap)
  - Name, description, connection status `Badge` (success for connected, default for not connected)
  - Connect/Disconnect `Button` (primary variant for connect, danger for disconnect)
- Toggle connection state on button click
- Empty state when no integrations match filter

### 3. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\PaymentSetup.tsx` (11.5 KB)
- Page title "Payment Setup" with subtitle
- **Stripe section** (Card): Stripe-colored icon, connection status badge, account ID `stripe_acct_xxx` when connected, Connect/Disconnect button
- **PayPal section** (Card): PayPal-colored icon, not connected status, "Connect PayPal" primary button
- **Payout Settings** (Card): payout schedule dropdown (daily/weekly/monthly), minimum payout amount input with `$` prefix, currency selector (USD/EUR/GBP/CAD/AUD)
- **Tax Settings** (Card): collect taxes toggle, conditional tax rate input (%) that appears when taxes enabled
- **Transaction Fees info** (Card): info icon with two lines showing Creator plan (5%) and Creator Pro plan (0%)

### 4. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\Account.tsx` (12.5 KB)
- Page title "Account" with subtitle
- **Profile section** (Card): avatar preview with upload button, full name input, email input (disabled with helper text), username input with `stan.io/` prefix
- **Password section** (Card): current password, new password, confirm password inputs, "Update Password" button
- **Plan section** (Card): current plan badge ("Creator Pro" accent badge), billing cycle info, 8 plan features with green checkmarks in a 2-column grid, "Manage Plan" button
- **Notification Preferences** (Card): three toggles with icons and descriptions - Email Notifications, Marketing Emails, Weekly Digest - separated by dividers
- **Danger Zone** (Card with red border): warning icon, detailed warning text about permanent deletion, "Delete Account" danger button

### 5. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\App.tsx` (3.0 KB)
- Imports `React`, `useState`, `Suspense`, `lazy`
- Imports `ThemeProvider` from `./lib/theme-context`
- Imports `AppShell` from `./components/layout/AppShell`
- Imports `demoUser` from `./lib/demo-data`
- **19 lazy-loaded page imports** for all pages (Dashboard through Account)
- **`PAGE_MAP`**: `Record<string, React.LazyExoticComponent<React.ComponentType<any>>>` mapping 19 page IDs to their lazy components
- **`LoadingSpinner`**: centered spinner using `var(--accent)` border color with CSS animation
- **`App` component**: `useState` for `currentView` (default 'dashboard'), derives `ActivePage` from `PAGE_MAP`, returns `ThemeProvider` > `AppShell` > `Suspense` > `<ActivePage />`
- AppShell receives `activePage`, `onPageChange`, `user` (name + avatar from demoUser), and `plan`
- Default export `App`
